steps.title: Substitute the point
steps.content:
Since $(8,c)$ lies on the circle, substitute $x=8$ and $y=c$ into the equation:
$$(8-4)^2+(c-7)^2=16$$

steps.title: Simplify the equation
steps.content:
Compute the squared term from the $x$-coordinate:
$$16+(c-7)^2=16$$
Subtract $16$ from both sides:
$$(c-7)^2=0$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content:
The only number whose square is $0$ is $0$, so
$$c-7=0$$
$$c=7$$
The answer is 7.
